perturbation.original_text: (a) The Remarketing Agent may resign and be discharged from its duties and obligations hereunder with respect to the VRRM-MFP Shares by giving 90 days' prior written notice to the Fund and the Calculation and Paying Agent.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) The Remarketing Agent may resign and be discharged from its duties and obligations hereunder with respect to the VRRM-MFP Shares by giving 10 days' prior written notice to the Fund and the Calculation and Paying Agent.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the notice period for resignation could be problematic under certain regulations or common law principles requiring reasonable notice, particularly if finding a replacement takes longer.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Implied Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing
perturbation.law_explanation: Reducing the notice period to 10 days may violate the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing under New York law (where the contract is governed) because it may not provide the Fund with sufficient time to find a replacement Remarketing Agent, potentially harming the Fund's interests. The UCC requires parties to act honestly and reasonably, and 10 days may be deemed insufficient in this context.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The modification reduces the notice period from 90 days to 10 days. The Perturbation Explanation states this could violate regulations or common law, especially if finding a replacement takes longer. The Law Explanation suggests this violates the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing under New York law, potentially harming the Fund's interests. Scraped Law Snippet 1 states that every contract within the UCC imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance, suggesting the reduced notice period could be seen as a breach of this obligation, because finding a replacement might not be feasible in 10 days.

perturbation.original_text: (b) The Fund may remove the Remarketing Agent with respect to the VRRM-MFP Shares by giving at least 60 days' prior written notice to the Remarketing Agent (and will provide prior notice also to the Calculation and Paying Agent, if any); provided, however, that no such removal shall become effective for an additional 30 days unless the Fund shall have appointed at least one nationally recognized securities dealer with expertise in remarketing variable rate securities as a successor Remarketing Agent for the VRRM-MFP Shares and the successor Remarketing Agent shall have entered into a remarketing agreement with the Fund, in form and substance satisfactory to the Fund, in which it shall have agreed to, among other duties, conduct remarketings in respect of VRRM-MFP Shares and determine the Dividend Rate on each Business Day for the VRRM-MFP Shares in accordance with the terms and conditions of the Supplement.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) The Fund may remove the Remarketing Agent with respect to the VRRM-MFP Shares immediately with written notice to the Remarketing Agent (and will provide prior notice also to the Calculation and Paying Agent, if any).
perturbation.explanation: Removing the 60-day notice period and the 30-day grace period for finding a replacement Remarketing Agent may be problematic under regulations or common law principles requiring reasonable notice, particularly if the abrupt change harms the Remarketing Agent.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Implied Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing
perturbation.law_explanation: Removing the notice period entirely could violate the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing under New York law (where the contract is governed) because it allows the Fund to terminate the Remarketing Agent without any opportunity to mitigate damages or wind down operations, potentially harming the Remarketing Agent's interests. The UCC requires parties to act honestly and reasonably, and immediate termination may be deemed unreasonable in this context.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a 60-day written notice period before the Fund could remove the Remarketing Agent and a 30-day grace period. The changed text eliminates both notice periods, allowing for immediate removal with written notice. The Perturbation Explanation suggests this may violate principles requiring reasonable notice. The Law Explanation states this could violate the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Scraped Law Snippet 1 states, "Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement." By removing the notice period entirely, the changed text contradicts the obligation of good faith, as it allows termination without opportunity for the Remarketing Agent to mitigate damages. This constitutes a legal contradiction as the changed text undermines the legal obligation of good faith.

perturbation.original_text: (a) This Agreement shall terminate as to the Remarketing Agent and its obligations hereunder with respect to VRRM-MFP Shares upon the earliest to occur of (a) the effective date of the resignation or removal of such Remarketing Agent pursuant to Section 5(a) and Section 5(b), respectively, (b) the completion of a successful Transition Remarketing on a New Mode Commencement Date in connection with transition to a new Mode, or (c) the date on which no VRRM-MFP Shares are Outstanding.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) This Agreement shall terminate as to the Remarketing Agent and its obligations hereunder with respect to VRRM-MFP Shares immediately upon written notice from the Fund.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the termination conditions to allow immediate termination upon notice from the Fund, without cause, is a significant alteration that could be challenged under common law principles requiring good faith and fair dealing.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Implied Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing
perturbation.law_explanation: Allowing termination at will with immediate effect may violate the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing under New York law, as it deprives the Remarketing Agent of the benefit of its bargain without any recourse, particularly if significant investments or preparations were made in reliance on the agreement's original terms. This modification disregards the requirement for parties to act honestly and reasonably, potentially constituting a breach of contract under New York law.

perturbation.justification: The modification in the contract allows the Fund to terminate the agreement immediately upon written notice, which contradicts the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Scraped Law Snippet 1 states, 'Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement.' By allowing termination at will, the modified contract potentially deprives the Remarketing Agent of the benefit of its bargain without recourse, especially if significant investments were made based on the original agreement's terms. This undermines the obligation of good faith, establishing a contradiction.
